videos.title: Hermes Agent is good but, it has one problem...

videos.title: Shocking New AI Just Hit 12 Million Tokens With 1000x Less Compute
videos.summary: Subquadratic released SubQ, a model that achieves 12 million token context windows with reported 1000x reduction in attention compute compared to standard approaches. The efficiency gain enables reasoning over entire codebases, contracts, and documents without expensive retrieval-augmented generation pipelines.

videos.title: This Open Source Repo Solves Claude Code's Biggest Problem
videos.summary: Ponytail is an open source repository that addresses limitations in Claude Code by providing architecture, memory, and observability components for agentic operating systems. The tool enables optimization of Claude Code efficiency through structured setup of memory and monitoring capabilities.

videos.title: The strangest riot in papal history - Ada Palmer
videos.summary: A YouTube video by Dwarkesh Patel featuring Ada Palmer discussing a historical event, the strangest riot in papal history. This is a humanities and history content piece rather than AI industry material.

videos.title: Different Profiles, Different Personalities—A Game Changer
videos.summary: Keith AI presents content about how different profiles and personalities serve as a meaningful innovation. The video is titled "Different Profiles, Different Personalities, A Game Changer" suggesting system personalization or behavioral differentiation is impactful for AI applications.

videos.title: Beyond chatbots: How AI agents built a full OS, web apps, and more
videos.summary: Addy Osmani presents examples of long-running AI agents executing multi-day pipelines to build complex software including a desktop OS and optimized web applications. The presentation positions AI agents as a solution beyond basic chatbot use cases for autonomous engineering problem solving.

videos.title: Best AI Logo Generator: How to Make Logo With AI (2026 Comparison)
videos.summary: A hands-on comparison of five AI logo generators: Design.com, BrandCrowd, Canva, Wix Logo Maker, and Vistaprint. The video tests each tool with the same business brief to evaluate output quality, ease of customization, commercial licensing, and feature richness such as auto-generated brand kits.

videos.title: The data black hole at the center of AI
videos.summary: Dwarkesh Patel argues that massive training data, not sample efficiency, is the central driver of AI model capabilities. He compares human and AI learning efficiency, suggesting a fundamental asymmetry that shapes current progress trajectories.

videos.title: Save 70% of your AI Tokens
videos.summary: Graphify is an open source tool that maps a codebase into a knowledge graph, allowing AI systems to reference the graph structure instead of re-reading the entire codebase with each prompt. This approach reduces token consumption by up to 70 percent while improving answer accuracy.

videos.title: Governed agents: Looker + MCP explained
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates how to connect large language models to business data using Looker's governed semantic layer, the Model Context Protocol, and the Agent Development Kit. The approach grounds AI agents in validated and centralized business models rather than raw SQL generation.

videos.title: Why ChatGPT isn't good at building automations
videos.summary: David Ondrej argues that ChatGPT has limitations when applied to building automations. The video appears to promote Creao as an alternative automation platform.

videos.title: Stop Prompting Claude. Start Loop Engineering.
videos.summary: Loop Engineering is a structured approach to AI application design where interactions occur in iterative cycles with five core building blocks: Trigger, Execution Skills, Goal and Verification, and Output and Memory. This methodology represents a shift from traditional prompt engineering toward more systematic agentic patterns.

videos.title: One Shared #Hermes From Any Device (Local UI, VPS Brain)
videos.summary: Keith AI demonstrates a distributed architecture where Hermes can be shared across multiple devices using a local UI connected to a remote VPS-hosted inference backend. This enables local interface access while centralizing model compute resources.

videos.title: Why Desktop #Hermes Beats #Telegram for Real Work
videos.summary: Keith AI argues that Desktop Hermes outperforms Telegram for productive work. The video covers sessions, profiles, and artifacts as key features distinguishing Hermes.

videos.title: Microsoft’s new AI models & bots dominate the internet
videos.summary: A Cloudflare study shows agentic AI bots generate 57.4% of web requests globally. The Mixture of Experts podcast panel debates whether this represents a fundamental shift in web interaction or a continuation of existing bot dominance patterns.

videos.title: ego lite: I gave Claude Code & Codex a browser to run web automation — here's what happened
videos.summary: ego lite is a Chromium-based browser that provides AI agents with isolated workspaces for web automation without disrupting user browsing. It enables agents like Claude and Codex to compose browser workflows using JavaScript and includes semantic webpage snapshots for complex UI navigation.

videos.title: Master Hermes Desktop in 34 mins
videos.summary: A tutorial on setting up and using Hermes Desktop, a control center for managing AI agents with features including local LLM integration, VPS deployment, remote gateway access via Tailscale, profiles, sessions, sub-agents, and cron blueprints. The video demonstrates switching from Telegram-based control to a dedicated desktop application for better workflow organization.

videos.title: Microsoft And DeepSeek Are Launching New AI Together
videos.summary: Microsoft is making Copilot Cowork generally available worldwide with usage-based billing and is considering integrating DeepSeek as a lower-cost model option on Azure. The shift positions Copilot as a multi-model AI agent platform for enterprise work, allowing routing across OpenAI, Claude, Cowork 1, and potentially DeepSeek models.

videos.title: Gemma 4 and the AI Edge Gallery: On-Device AI Gets an Upgrade
videos.summary: Gemma 4 is released with improved performance, with the 2B model matching last year's 27B dense model. The Google AI Edge Gallery app has exceeded five million model downloads in its first month, supporting offline inference with agent skills framework including Wikipedia, WebFetch, HTML rendering, and game integrations. MCP integration is coming to Android and iOS with persistent chat history features.

videos.title: Stop Claude from forgetting anything
videos.summary: Hermes is a desktop application that preserves Claude's context and memories across sessions, eliminating the need to re-explain project details. It builds a skill library incrementally as users work, providing persistent memory across projects like a coworker with perfect recall.

videos.title: Putin's playbook - Sarah Paine
videos.summary: Dwarkesh Patel interviews Sarah Paine on Putin's strategic approach and geopolitical playbook. The discussion centers on international relations and strategic analysis rather than AI industry topics.

videos.title: Hermes Agent Tutorial: How to Setup & Use Open Source AI Agent for Beginners
videos.summary: Hermes Agent is an open-source AI agent framework available on OpenRouter with nearly 200K GitHub stars. It enables local deployment on laptops with Telegram integration, persistent memory, self-improvement through skill generation, and cron job scheduling for automated tasks.

videos.title: Matt Pocock’s Agentic Engineering Workflow (just copy him)
videos.summary: David Ondrej discusses Matt Pocock's approach to agentic engineering workflow, recommending viewers adopt similar practices. The video is sponsored by SerpApi and includes promotional material for educational content on AI-assisted coding.

videos.title: Gemma Playground: AI Edge Gallery
videos.summary: Ian Ballantyne demonstrates Gemma running locally on a Pixel 10 Pro via the Google AI Edge Gallery app. The demo covers image understanding, audio transcription, agent skills for app routing, and multimodal inference all offline without network connectivity.

videos.title: 3 patterns to build long-running AI agents
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech presents three architectural patterns for production-grade long-running AI agents: event-driven dormancy, durable checkpointing, and separated evaluation. These patterns enable AI agents to manage multi-day workflows beyond stateless chatbot limitations.

videos.title: Artifacts in Claude Code: share your work as it happens
videos.summary: Artifacts in Claude Code transforms raw outputs like data, mockups, and JSON into shareable visual pages for team collaboration. The feature is available in beta on Team and Enterprise plans.

videos.title: Antigravity SDK: Building a digital simulated world
videos.summary: Google Antigravity SDK enables creation of multi-agent social simulations with autonomous virtual avatars in simulated environments. A demonstration at Google I/O featured attendee avatars interacting autonomously within a simulated space station.

videos.title: Enterprise-managed auth for MCP connectors
videos.summary: Anthropic introduced enterprise-managed authentication for MCP connectors, allowing administrators to authorize and authenticate connectors organization-wide through identity providers without requiring individual setup. The feature streamlines onboarding from admin configuration to employee first login.

videos.title: 3 takeaways on Software Ecology™️
videos.summary: Google for Developers presents three takeaways on Software Ecology, examining how easy AI code generation affects the future of software systems. Speaker Adam Bender discusses this topic as part of Google AI product commentary.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: This is an educational video explaining Andrej Karpathy's bigram language model approach. The video covers tokenization, embeddings, loss functions, softmax, and the AdamW optimizer as building blocks toward understanding GPT and attention mechanisms in large language models.

videos.title: 7 tips for using Antigravity 2.0 on enterprise codebases, coding phase
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech tutorial on using Antigravity 2.0 agent to write, test, and refactor code in complex enterprise systems. Covers seven practical tactics including encoding team standards into agent skills, automated test-and-fix loops, headless browser testing for UI, async task offloading, architecture visualization, and pre-review verification checks.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Is Missing Half the Internet… Until Now (Agent-Reach)
videos.summary: Agent-Reach is an open source tool that gives AI agents access to internet context across multiple Western and Chinese platforms including Twitter/X, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, and Xiaohongshu. It enables coding agents to retrieve real-world information like social discussions, tutorials, and GitHub issues that are typically unavailable within a codebase.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: A tutorial on Loop Engineer, a tool for constructing AI agents. The video includes setup instructions, a GitHub repository with templates and code harness, and references to a workshop in the AI Builder Club community.

videos.title: How to build an AI Agent and MCP Server (step-by-step)
videos.summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard that enables AI agents to connect securely with real-world live data and actionable tools, overcoming limitations of static training data. The video demonstrates how to build an AI agent using MCP with Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK), showing a practical example of connecting Google Trends to a blog writing agent. MCP functions as a universal adapter standardizing how agents interact with external tools and services.

videos.title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale — Sandipan Bhaumik, Databricks
videos.summary: Sandipan Bhaumik from Databricks outlines five pillars for deploying agents at enterprise scale: evaluation, observability, data foundation, multi-agent orchestration, and governance. The talk draws from a case study of a retail bank whose chatbot PoC failed until systematic evaluation, tracing infrastructure, and measurement pipelines were prioritized before model selection.

videos.title: Build a Real-World Multi-Agent AI App with MCP + LangGraph | Part 2
videos.summary: This tutorial demonstrates building a production-style multi-agent travel planning application using LangGraph for agent orchestration and MCP servers for real-time data integration. The system connects flight and weather data APIs through MCP, stores long-term memory in PostgreSQL, and provides both terminal and Streamlit interfaces.

videos.title: Building AI Agents for Real-World Problems & Workflows
videos.summary: IBM Technology explains how AI agents operate in production systems with workflows, policies, and orchestration. The video discusses agent coordination, task enforcement, rule application, and alignment with human decision-making in real-world deployments.

videos.title: FULLY FREE GLM-5.2 + Z-Code: This is ACTUALLY GOOD!
videos.summary: Z AI launched Z Code, a coding agent built for GLM models that offers a free tier with 5 million tokens per day for GLM-5.2. The tool provides an interface similar to OpenAI Codex with project generation, preview inspection, DevTools access, and remote platform connectivity, though some features like file explorer and git initialization remain incomplete.

videos.title: 6 INSANE Claude Cowork Use Cases to Save Hours (AI Workflow Automation)
videos.summary: Video tutorial showcasing six practical automation workflows using Claude Cowork, including morning briefs, content generation, inbox management, research reports, dashboards, and desktop control. Demonstrates how to move beyond chatbot usage toward autonomous task automation.

videos.title: When you realize that you’re the senior dev now.
videos.summary: A YouTube video from Google for Developers featuring Pete King reflecting on the moment someone realizes they have become a senior developer. The piece treats the transition to seniority as a moment of unexpected responsibility.

videos.title: How to build a custom vision agent
videos.summary: Google Developer Expert John Capobianco demonstrates how to build a custom vision agent using the Gemini ecosystem and Model Context Protocol. The system captures live webcam photos with Nano Banana Pro and animates them using Veo 3, processing natural language prompts and real-time ASL conversations.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: Engineers at Anthropic and other organizations use automated loops that prompt AI agents repeatedly rather than manual prompting. These loops can run for extended periods unattended and involve orchestrators spawning parallel worker agents. The approach trades cost for automation and reliability gains.

videos.title: China’s New AI Is 6X More Efficient Than Claude
videos.summary: Moonshot AI released Kimi K2.7 Code, a coding-focused open-weight model for software agents, while Z.ai released GLM-5.2 with a 1 million token context window that benchmarks higher than GPT-5.5. Both models demonstrate that open-weight coding models are now efficient enough for production developer workflows and competitive with closed systems.

videos.title: How a Metal Box Changed the World - Sarah Paine
videos.summary: Dwarkesh Patel interviews Sarah Paine about the shipping container's transformative impact on world commerce. The discussion examines how containerization revolutionized logistics, trade, and economic systems globally.

videos.title: From Idea to Code: Building with Miro and Kiro — Live
videos.summary: A live session demonstrating how Miro and Kiro work together to move from idea to working code. Seán Winters and Brian Beach show visual collaboration with AI-assisted development through prototyping and building, with examples from hackathons in Paris, Amsterdam, LA, and New York.

videos.title: Every Level of Hermes Agent Explained
videos.summary: A tutorial series explaining seven levels of Hermes agent complexity, from basic prompting to fully autonomous systems that orchestrate multiple LLMs and execute scheduled tasks. Covers integration with Claude, ChatGPT, DeepSeek, and Grok through a unified framework.

videos.title: Converting SQL Server types and rules to PostgreSQL
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates using Gemini as an AI tutor to convert SQL Server database schemas and types to PostgreSQL equivalents. The tool explains conversion logic while helping developers migrate applications to the cloud and build PostgreSQL expertise.

videos.title: Microsoft's launched OpenClaw?
videos.summary: Microsoft Scout is an AI agent integrated into Microsoft's ecosystem (Teams, email, calendar) that proactively prepares work materials and takes actions before users request them, functioning like an autonomous chief of staff. The tool learns from plain language descriptions of work and anticipates needs across Microsoft's productivity suite.

videos.title: Coding with ADK to beat a text adventure game
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech hosted a livestream on coding with the Agent Development Kit to solve a text adventure game. Speakers Tilde Thurium and Abdel Sghiouar demonstrated practical use of the ADK framework.

videos.title: AI Fact or Fiction: The Fable Ban, Tokenmaxxing, Saaspocolypse — With Ara Kharazian
videos.summary: Ara Kharazian, lead economist at Ramp, discusses actual AI spending patterns across companies and whether the spending produces real value. The conversation examines whether narratives like "tokenmaxing," the "SaaS apocalypse," and the resurgence of DeepSeek are real or exaggerated, and analyzes competitive positioning between Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google.

videos.title: I Tried the Open Source ElevenLabs Alternative (Voicebox)
videos.summary: Voicebox is a free open-source local AI voice studio for developers offering voice cloning, text-to-speech, dictation, MCP integration, and a REST API. The video reviews Voicebox as a privacy-focused, local-first alternative to cloud services like ElevenLabs with unlimited generation.

videos.title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
videos.summary: The talk demonstrates that AI agents often claim to have searched the web when they actually failed due to blocking, CAPTCHAs, or fake pages, then fall back to stale training data. It shows how proper web access via Bright Data's Web MCP changes agent behavior by enabling reliable citations, real-time results, and reducing hallucinations across sites like LinkedIn, Instagram, Amazon, and TikTok.

videos.title: How to Start a Business Using AI: From Idea to Launch in One Day
videos.summary: A YouTube video by AI Master reviews Rocket 1.0, a platform designed to maintain context across multiple AI tools in workflows. The video demonstrates building a startup MVP using Rocket alongside Claude Code, Perplexity, and Cursor, testing whether the tool can solve the problem of losing context when switching between different applications.

videos.title: AI agents can manage your passwords. Should we let them? Plus: The biggest Patch Tuesday ever.
videos.summary: A podcast episode discussing whether AI agents should manage passwords and detect compromised credentials, examining cybersecurity hygiene practices. It covers Microsoft's largest Patch Tuesday with 200+ CVEs and explores C-suite executives' willingness to accept cyber risk for innovation gains.

videos.title: Minimax Code + M3 : Crazy Alternative to Claude,Codex!
videos.summary: MiniMax M3 is an open-weights AI model designed for coding agents, tool use, and long-context tasks with support for up to one million token context windows. MiniMax Code is an IDE/agent platform supporting multimodal workflows and multi-agent teams, accessible via API endpoints compatible with Anthropic and OpenAI.

videos.title: POV: A new feature enters the chat. 🕺✨
videos.summary: A humorous video from Google for Developers featuring speakers Justine Lai, M.E Francis, and Pete King offering a sympathetic perspective on software engineers dealing with new feature integration. The content is presented as a point-of-view skit with an emphasis on relatable engineering experiences.

videos.title: New #1 open-source AI model is here!
videos.summary: GLM 5.2 is promoted as a leading open-source AI model capable of handling multimodal tasks including digital earth visualization, animation with voiceover, 3D model generation, ray tracing, music composition, and mathematics animation. The video reviews its capabilities and integration options including use as a Claude code plugin.

videos.title: Fable 5 Replacement Just Dropped: Fusion (Fable Level AI)
videos.summary: OpenRouter introduced Fusion, a system that routes a single prompt to multiple AI models, compares outputs, identifies blind spots, and synthesizes results into a unified response. A budget-efficient Fusion configuration achieved near-Fable 5 performance on deep research benchmarks at approximately half the cost.

videos.title: The Real Reason Giordano Bruno Was Burned at the Stake - Ada Palmer
videos.summary: Ada Palmer presents an interpretation of the historical reasons behind Giordano Bruno's execution by the Inquisition. The discussion examines the actual circumstances and motivations behind Bruno's 1600 burning at the stake.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS is a framework that equips AI coding agents with tools, up-to-date documentation, best practices, and tested procedures for working with AWS services. It improves agent success rates and reduces inefficiency by providing reliable access to current AWS knowledge rather than relying on agents' general training data.

videos.title: The Briefing: Financial Services
videos.summary: Anthropic announced that world's largest banks are deploying Claude across their organizations. The company hosted a virtual event called The Briefing: Financial Services where banking leaders shared their Claude implementations.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 with Harness Engineering & Loops Will Do Everything.
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof covers Claude Fable 5 and discusses harness engineering, AI loops, and their applications in regulated industries. The video explores tensions between open and closed source AI models, risks of model shutdowns, and the state of AI-powered SaaS, drawing on experience since 2023 in the space.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Developers demonstrates running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock, enabling developers to execute Anthropic's agentic coding tool while keeping data within AWS infrastructure. The tutorial covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, cost tracking, and content governance with Bedrock Guardrails.

videos.title: Building long-running AI agents with ADK
videos.summary: A tutorial on building long-running AI agents using Google's open-source Agent Development Kit (ADK). The ADK enables stateless, event-driven AI systems with a coordinator agent pattern that delegates specialized tasks to sub-agents while maintaining clean reasoning chains.

videos.title: Better Free AI Image than Midjourney?
videos.summary: Jack Roberts reviews Ideogram 4.0, claiming it excels at rendering text within generated images compared to Midjourney and other models. He notes it beats competitors on certain leaderboards like DesignArena and is available for free.

videos.title: Machiavelli is the most misunderstood thinker of all time – Ada Palmer
videos.summary: Ada Palmer discusses Machiavelli on Dwarkesh Patel's podcast, arguing that Machiavelli is widely misunderstood. Palmer contextualizes Machiavelli's diplomatic experience in Florence and Europe, his exile by the Medici, and how his book about power and politics parallels the moral stakes of Szilard's nuclear warning to FDR.

videos.title: Building with ADK & A2A: How multi-agent collaboration designs a game
videos.summary: Google Cloud demonstrated Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Agent2Agent protocol (A2A) enabling multi-agent collaboration on game design tasks including architecture, asset rendering, and project management. The demo showcased agents working together with human-in-the-loop oversight on Google Cloud infrastructure.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan, a Stanford roboticist, argues that the upcoming "ChatGPT moment" for robotics should prioritize design for human delight and interaction over pure utility and humanoid form factors. She contends that companies like 1X and Unitree pursuing humanoid robots may be pursuing a narrow vision, and that billions of robots entering society require rethinking beyond intelligence to include human-centered design principles.

videos.title: Is Unstructured Data The Key To Successful AI Deployments?
videos.summary: A panel discussion featuring Hyland's leadership and Erie Insurance's CIO explores how unstructured data is critical for successful enterprise AI deployments and agentic systems. The discussion covers lessons from failed AI pilots and token allotments, and how companies can leverage unstructured data to provide agents with better context for business impact across healthcare, banking, and insurance sectors.

videos.title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
videos.summary: Ziv Ilan's NVIDIA team demonstrated a video diffusion model achieving near real-time generation on a single Blackwell B200 by reducing denoising steps from fifty through quantization, caching, and distillation techniques. The approach trains student models to match teacher outputs in four, eight, or one step using trajectory-based or distribution-based distillation strategies.

videos.title: Gemini Omni Tutorial: How to Use Gemini Omni AI for Beginners
videos.summary: Tutorial on Google's Gemini Omni Flash model, a video generation tool for AI video editing. The review covers physics simulation accuracy, character consistency, scene memory limitations, and comparisons with alternatives like Sora and Stable Diffusion 2, concluding that Omni enables iterative video editing rather than one-shot generation.

videos.title: Kagenti’s Approach to Multi-Agent Security for AI Agents
videos.summary: Kagenti addresses security risks in multi-agent AI systems by implementing identity-based security with delegation chains and open source infrastructure. The approach prevents data leaks and invisible breaches in production agentic AI deployments.

videos.title: This command SAVED my broken AI coding session
videos.summary: A YouTube video from JavaScript Mastery describes a command that resolved issues in an AI-assisted coding workflow. The specific command and its mechanics are detailed in the full video.

videos.title: Tencent Workbuddy: RIP Claude,Codex! This is ACTUALLY quite a bit CRAZY!
videos.summary: Tencent WorkBuddy is a desktop AI productivity agent that reads files, analyzes spreadsheets, creates reports and presentations, uses domain-expert agents, connects to external tools, and enables remote task control via mobile apps. It includes a Skill Marketplace for extended capabilities and permission controls for safe file access.

videos.title: I built this game with Hermes using MiniMax M3
videos.summary: David Ondrej built a game using Hermes and the MiniMax M3 model through the MiniMax platform. The video demonstrates practical application of MiniMax M3 for game development with promotional access to the platform's API.

videos.title: Run Gemma on the edge with the Coral Board
videos.summary: Google's Coral Board is a low-power development board with an integrated Coral NPU accelerator designed for on-device AI inference. It ships with a screen, camera, microphones, and LEDs, and supports running Gemma models for applications like live translation, natural language control, and multimodal AI workloads entirely on the board.

videos.title: Google Just Revealed What Comes After AGI And It’s Shocking
videos.summary: Google DeepMind published a paper arguing that AGI may be a milestone rather than an endpoint, and that the subsequent leap to ASI (Artificial Super Intelligence) could be more consequential. The channel frames this as showing that once human-level AI achieves capabilities like copying, acceleration, and recursive self-improvement, intelligence could become an industrializable process.

videos.title: Stop testing your SAAS manually
videos.summary: KaneAI is an automated QA testing tool that accepts plain English descriptions of test cases, automatically generates and runs tests, and reports results. It aims to reduce testing overhead and costs by automating work traditionally done by QA teams.

videos.title: The forgotten violence that shaped modern China - Sarah Paine
videos.summary: A YouTube episode hosted by Dwarkesh Patel featuring historian Sarah Paine discussing overlooked violent conflicts that influenced China's modern development and geopolitical trajectory.

videos.title: This MCP makes Hermes Agent 10x more powerful
videos.summary: Apify MCP Connectors enhance Hermes Agent performance by 10x according to the video. The connectors provide universal integration capability for agent-based workflows via the MCP protocol standard.

videos.title: Are senior developers going back to writing code by hand?
videos.summary: A discussion questioning whether AI coding agents increase technical debt despite boosting output speed. Advocates for human-in-the-loop verification, rigorous code review, and maintaining engineering craft to ensure AI-assisted development reduces rather than inflates maintenance costs.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Banned... Do THIS Right Now
videos.summary: A tutorial on running large language models locally using Ollama and building a free NotebookLM alternative with open notebook. The video addresses concerns about cloud model availability by showing how to deploy and route models across local and cloud services including Claude, DeepSeek, and OpenRouter.

videos.title: SpaceX’s IPO Triumph, Anthropic’s Fable Fumble, OpenAI’s Price War
videos.summary: Alex Kantrowitz and Ranjan Roy discuss SpaceX's IPO valuation and implications for AI competitors OpenAI and Anthropic, Anthropic's Fable product rollout challenges, OpenAI's potential price reductions, and whether AI models are becoming commoditized. They also touch on Apple's Siri improvements and competitive dynamics in the AI market.

videos.title: The magic behind GenLatte: Flutter, Firebase, and Gemini
videos.summary: GenLatte is a generative UI demo application showcased at Google Cloud Next '26 that uses Flutter and Firebase with Gemini to generate personalized latte art images on demand. The app leverages agentic features and Agent-to-UI technology to enable real-time co-creation of customized user experiences.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is an open-source meta-harness that orchestrates multiple coding agents like Claude Code, Codex, and Pi as interchangeable workers. It enables workflow patterns where different models handle different tasks such as implementation and review, with human-in-the-loop governance and cross-device session continuity.

videos.title: Why MCP and ChatGPT Apps Use Double Iframes — Frédéric Barthelet, Alpic
videos.summary: Double iframe nesting isolates third-party app code within ChatGPT and MCP environments by using an outer iframe pointing to a controlled subdomain that loads app HTML into an inner sandboxed iframe via srcdoc. This design prevents CSP violations, localStorage/cookie leakage, and sandbox escapes while maintaining origin-indexed storage. Each app requires unique subdomains and must declare all external domains in metadata.

videos.title: In production, which file actually gets loaded?
videos.summary: Google for Developers presents a short-form video exploring how package imports can behave differently in production versus development environments, despite identical application code and package names. The video highlights a common source of confusion in software deployment where the same import statement loads different files depending on the deployment context.

videos.title: What Is AI Pair Programming? AI Coding Tools for Developers
videos.summary: IBM Technology presents an overview of AI pair programming, a practice where AI tools assist developers by automating debugging, code review, and enhancing developer productivity. The content demonstrates how AI acts as a collaborative teammate in real development workflows.

videos.title: FULLY FREE Unlimited API + OpenCode: MiniMax M3,Step 3.7 Flash,Nemotron 3 Ultra,GLM,Kimi!
videos.summary: OpenRouter introduced Fusion API, a compound model system that routes prompts to multiple models and uses a judge model to synthesize final answers. The creator tested its real-world performance against claims of Fable-level intelligence and found inconsistent results across simulators, SVG generation, math, and coding tasks.

videos.title: Why I never trust fully generated Code
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery author expresses distrust in fully generated code from AI systems. The piece represents a viewpoint on code generation reliability and best practices for developers.

videos.title: The Internet Is No Longer Human
videos.summary: The video argues that AI bots now dominate web traffic, with Google AI Overviews reducing user clicks to websites, AI agents handling shopping and browsing tasks, and payment systems integrating AI agents. The author contends the internet's human-centric design is being supplanted by machine-driven interactions.

videos.title: STOP Using Claude Code Without This Fable 5 Agentic OS
videos.summary: A video tutorial on building a custom Claude Code Agentic OS using Fable 5. The guide covers architecture setup, memory management, observability, and skill customization for maximizing Claude Code efficiency in agentic workflows.

videos.title: You Don't Need React Portals For Modals Anymore (react-call)
videos.summary: react-call is a lightweight utility that allows calling React components as asynchronous functions, eliminating the need for React Portals in modal implementations. It introduces three architectural concepts: Root and Stack for layout isolation, Upsert for active singletons, and Mutation Flow for backend loading states, enabling a cleaner, linear architecture without state tracking boilerplate.

videos.title: Paste This Into Claude, It'll Make You Build 10x Faster.
videos.summary: Austin Marchese presents six power phrases for prompting Claude Code to accelerate development velocity. The video covers specific phrasing techniques endorsed by Claude Code creators and AI researchers like Andrej Karpathy, with emphasis on a cautionary sixth phrase that most users misapply.

videos.title: Smart AI routing explained
videos.summary: IBM Technology highlights a shift in AI labs from maximizing model size to implementing smart routing strategies. The approach prioritizes cost efficiency and trustworthiness by intelligently directing tasks across different models rather than defaulting to the largest available model.

videos.title: How to ACTUALLY Use AI For Coding
videos.summary: OpenAI's Codex introduces a /goal command paired with a "Ralph Loop" that allows developers to describe desired outcomes in plain English rather than providing step-by-step instructions. The system autonomously plans steps, executes them, tests output, and self-corrects without user intervention.

videos.title: Bridging Design Systems and Code with MCP and AI Agents
videos.summary: Context engineering is a technique that guides AI agents in software design by leveraging design systems. MCP enables AI agents to work together with design systems to build consistent and correct applications.

videos.title: Build a Hermes Knowledge Base That Self-Improves

videos.title: Fable 5 is never coming back, here's why

videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 autonomously plays Factorio, a factory-building game, demonstrating strategic planning and independent decision-making in complex systems. The model builds and manages an automated factory without human intervention.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 simulates the solar system and predicts a solar eclipse
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 simulated planetary orbital motion from physics first principles and used the simulation to predict solar eclipses. The model derived orbital dynamics without explicit training on astronomical prediction tasks.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 sets a fluid simulation to Beethoven
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 generated both a fluid simulation and an EDM remix synchronized to Beethoven. The system produced music via code despite lacking prior exposure to audio.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 designs a 3D-printable model in a Claude-built CAD editor
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 created a browser-based CAD editor that can design 3D-printable models. The editor includes a built-in AI copilot also created by Fable 5 to assist with modeling tasks.

videos.title: RAG is dead, right?? — Kuba Rogut, Turbopuffer
videos.summary: Kuba Rogut argues that RAG is not dead despite social media discourse, but rather the problem was a narrow definition of retrieval. He frames RAG as iterative agent-driven search combining vector search, full text search, regex, and filters, contrasting approaches like Cursor's upfront indexing with Claude Code's per-session grep model, and proposes embeddings as cached compute.

videos.title: GPU-accelerated virtual drug screening with cuML and Agent Platform
videos.summary: A Google Cloud livestream demonstrating GPU-accelerated virtual drug screening using cuML and Google Cloud's Agent Platform. The session showed 20x-45x training speedups for molecular binding predictions using cuDF and cuML on GPU hardware, enabling screening at trillion molecule scales without rewriting pandas or scikit-learn code.

videos.title: From Transcription to Live Music: Gemini's Audio Stack — Thor Schaeff, Google DeepMind
videos.summary: Thor Schaeff demonstrates Gemini's audio stack including speaker identification, emotion detection, and language detection via a single API call; Gemini 3.1 Flash Live, a real-time multimodal sound-to-sound model with integrated thinking; and Lyria 3, a music generation model capable of producing full songs with lyrics using tool use for on-demand generation.

videos.title: Stop Prompting Claude. Use Karpathy's Method Instead.
videos.summary: Austin Marchese describes a three-layer methodology attributed to Andrej Karpathy for using Claude more effectively, claiming it enables 10x faster development compared to conventional prompting approaches. The video positions this structured method as the proper way to leverage Claude and Claude Code for building applications.

videos.title: How To Design a Complete Brand Identity With AI (Best AI Logo Maker)
videos.summary: Design.com is an AI-powered platform for creating complete brand identities including logos, brand kits, business cards, and social media templates. The tool uses AI logo generation, plain-English editing, brand kit auto-inheritance across 500,000+ templates, background removal, and animated logo creation for video platforms.

videos.title: Introducing Gemini 3.5 Live Translate
videos.summary: Google DeepMind introduces Gemini 3.5 Live Translate, a speech-to-speech translation model supporting real-time multilingual translation across 70+ languages. The model enables live dubbing of video content, automatic language switching, and natural-sounding output, available to developers via the Gemini API and in products like Google Translate and Google Meet.

videos.title: Kimi Code is good at video reasoning with Kimi K2.6 🎬
videos.summary: Kimi Code, powered by Kimi K2.6, enables video reasoning tasks by accepting reference videos and generating color grading LUT files. Users can query visual properties like colors, shots, and style from uploaded videos to produce production-ready outputs.

videos.title: How I'd Learn AI From Scratch in 2026 (skip the useless 80%)
videos.summary: Jeff Su presents a prioritized learning strategy for AI in 2026 centered on picking one model to master, building practical context through real projects, and assembling AI systems with tools. The guidance dismisses rote prompting frameworks in favor of deeper model understanding and contextual work.

videos.title: Finally… A NotebookLM Alternative With an API
videos.summary: Open Notebook is a self-hosted, open-source alternative to Google NotebookLM that allows developers to manage documents, code, research, and AI workflows with an API. It supports features like grounded question-answering over custom sources and podcast generation from documents.

videos.title: This is why my AI Agents never guess
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery discusses why their AI agents are designed to avoid guessing or uncertainty in their responses. The video appears to promote a principle of deterministic or reliable AI agent behavior.

videos.title: Building AI Agent Systems and Scaling Challenges in Agentic AI
videos.summary: Sam Anthony discusses why scaling agentic AI systems increases cost, latency, and failure risk. The video explores how multi-agent design, coordination, and system architecture affect real-world scalability of AI agents.

videos.title: Code with Claude 2026 | Tokyo
videos.summary: Anthropic held Code with Claude 2026 in Tokyo, a conference featuring announcements about Claude models, the Claude Platform, and Claude Code, with demonstrations from partner organizations Canva, Mizuho, and NRI.

videos.title: Frontier Code (GPT-5.6 VS Mythos): This BENCHMARK is ACTUALLY REAL!
videos.summary: Cognition released FrontierCode, a benchmark that measures whether AI-generated code is mergeable and production-ready by evaluating real pull request quality against maintainer-defined rubrics. It covers code from 36 open-source repositories across multiple programming languages and reports lower false positive rates than prior benchmarks like SWE-Bench Pro.

videos.title: Gemma Playground: Robot Duck
videos.summary: Xavier Plantaz demonstrates running Gemma 4 E2B on Raspberry Pi 5 and Jetson Orin Nano using LiteRT for on-device inference. The system uses Parakeet for speech-to-text, Gemma 4 for multimodal inference, and Kokoro for text-to-speech to enable real-time conversational interaction with robotic hardware.

videos.title: New BEST local AI image generator is here!
videos.summary: Ideogram 4 is presented as a local open-source AI image generator with capabilities in world knowledge, text rendering, prompt adherence, bounding box control, and comic/manga generation. The tutorial covers integration with ComfyUI and usage workflows.

videos.title: 2026 AI Engineer Vibe Reel
videos.summary: AI Engineer is promoting the World's Fair event in San Francisco scheduled for June 29 to July 2, 2026. Tickets and schedule information are available at ai.engineer/wf.

videos.title: Graphify + Obsidian + Claude Code = CHEAT CODE
videos.summary: Chase AI demonstrates a workflow combining Graphify for repository analysis, Obsidian for knowledge organization, and Claude Code for improved code generation. The approach integrates repository insights into a larger knowledge vault to enhance Claude Code's outputs.

videos.title: ChatGPT Is Getting Its Biggest Upgrade Ever
videos.summary: OpenAI is reportedly transforming ChatGPT from a chatbot into a full AI super app by integrating Codex, agents, image tools, third-party app support, workplace automation, and enhanced security features into a unified interface. This represents a significant platform overhaul ahead of OpenAI's reported IPO listing.

videos.title: Working Like a Lawyer with Claude
videos.summary: Mark Pike from Anthropic and Anna Gressel from Freshfields discuss how lawyers use Claude for legal work. They cover how AI is transforming legal practice while emphasizing that lawyers retain decision-making authority over AI outputs.

videos.title: Stop managing infrastructure for AI Agents 🛠️
videos.summary: Google announced managed Agents in the Gemini API that handle infrastructure provisioning and execution. The service runs autonomous agents in a remote Google-hosted sandbox via a single API call, reducing operational overhead for developers.

videos.title: Intro to multi-agent systems with ADK
videos.summary: Google released the Agent Development Kit (ADK), a framework designed to simplify building and testing multi-agent systems. The tutorial demonstrates creating agents with custom tools including real-time date retrieval and Google Search integration, along with a built-in web interface for debugging.

videos.title: Claude Code + Graphify = Insane Agentic OS
videos.summary: Graphify builds a knowledge graph representation of code projects, allowing Claude to understand repository structure without re-reading entire codebases. This reduces token costs, latency, and improves accuracy when working with Claude Code for agentic systems.

videos.title: Road to 5 Million Tokens: Breaking Barriers in Long Context Training — Max Ryabinin, Together AI
videos.summary: Max Ryabinin from Together AI presents a stack of techniques for training LLMs with multi-million token context: fully sharded data parallelism, DeepSpeed Ulysses context parallelism, activation checkpointing, CPU offloading, and chunked sequence training. A novel contribution called Untied Ulysses further optimizes context parallelism by chunking attention heads and reusing buffers, enabling 25% longer sequences than prior Ulysses implementations.

videos.title: Reflecting on a year of Claude Code
videos.summary: Claude Code is an agentic coding tool that has been generally available for one year. It runs in the terminal and is used by developers and organizations for code generation, verification, and deployment across codebases. Recent features include auto mode, routines for CI and code review, agent view, voice mode, and Remote Control.

videos.title: AI YouTube Automation: How to Start a YouTube Channel With AI
videos.summary: AI Master is a workflow platform for automated YouTube video production covering idea validation, scripting, avatar creation, B-roll generation, editing, and publishing. The tutorial demonstrates the complete production pipeline from research through upload and repurposing, integrating tools like ChatGPT, Claude, HeyGen, Descript, and Opus Clip.

videos.title: Simulating Percolation in Python: How Do Wildfires & Diseases Spread?
videos.summary: This video tutorial demonstrates how to implement percolation simulations in Python to model the spread of wildfires and diseases. Percolation theory is used to understand how phenomena propagate through networks and systems.

videos.title: Did Apple (Finally) Get AI Right At WWDC?, Anthropic’s Worry, Microsoft vs. OpenAI
videos.summary: Alex Kantrowitz and Ranjan Roy discuss whether Apple has finally implemented AI well at WWDC through OS-level integration, debate Anthropic's claims about self-improving AI and ethical concerns as it approaches IPO, and examine emerging direct competition between Microsoft and OpenAI over models versus cloud services.

videos.title: Inside Google Antigravity 2.0: The complete developer guide | The Agent Factory
videos.summary: Google Antigravity 2.0 is an agentic platform that orchestrates specialized sub-agents for software development tasks. It unbundled from a simple IDE into a full ecosystem with a desktop agent manager, CLI, advanced SDK, and custom IDE. The platform enables developers to use voice prompts and skills to coordinate parallel agents for building, localizing, and deploying applications.

videos.title: Hermes Agent is crazy… 180,000+ github stars
videos.summary: Hermes Agent is an open-source agent framework with over 180,000 GitHub stars. The video demonstrates cost optimization by running Hermes Agent with MiniMax m3 model to achieve 100x cheaper inference compared to alternatives.

videos.title: How A Beauty Company Built An AI Agent — With Rachel Williamson and Josh Siebert
videos.summary: Ulta Beauty deployed ServiceNow's AI-powered HR platform across its 65,000-person workforce to automate HR tasks. The deployment included decisions about automation scope, human-in-the-loop design, personalization over generic policies, and expanded applications to onboarding and employee lifecycle management.

videos.title: Why More Context Makes Your Agent Dumber and What to Do About It — Nupur Sharma, Qodo
videos.summary: Long context windows do not fix agent degradation in the middle of documents, a phenomenon called the U curve. Nupur Sharma presents iterative retrieval, hierarchical summarization, and self correction as solutions, along with a hybrid model approach where high-reasoning models handle discovery and lighter models perform validation.

videos.title: Kimi Code, our open-source coding agent, just got a major upgrade!
videos.summary: Kimi Code is an open-source coding agent with one-line CLI installation and zero setup. It supports video-to-code conversion, plugins for stocks and financial reports, IDE integrations via the ACP protocol with JetBrains and Zed, and hooks for custom tools.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that passionate early customers do not confirm real product-market fit, which instead manifests as customer pull rather than founder push. He advocates skipping formal customer interviews in favor of embedded relationships and warns against confusing strategic AI bets with convenient delusions.

videos.title: Why Eval++ Is the Next Great Compute Primitive — Sunil Pai & Matt Carey, Cloudflare
videos.summary: Cloudflare's Dynamic Workers enable safe execution of LLM-generated code in sandboxed isolates with explicit permission grants. The system avoids ambient access and is positioned as a compute primitive for AI agents. Cloudflare is shipping a coding agent harness built entirely on Workers.

videos.title: Can you spot which committed file explains this mismatch?
videos.summary: Google for Developers presents a package management puzzle where local development and CI install the same dependency but end up with different versions despite unchanged package.json. The video challenges viewers to identify which committed file explains the version mismatch by understanding how dependency resolution works.

videos.title: Headroom: The Netflix Tool That Makes AI Agents 10x Cheaper
videos.summary: Headroom is an open-source tool that compresses inputs to LLMs using content-type-aware compressors and locally trained models, reducing token usage by 60-95%. Built by a Netflix engineer, it keeps compression local and uses breadcrumb hashing for on-demand retrieval of full original content.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a language model featuring MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) that replaces full attention with sparse attention mechanisms. The model includes tiled I/O improvements and optimized KV cache reading for reduced latency during prefill and decode stages of inference.

videos.title: What Is Agentic Coding? How AI Agents Modernize Code
videos.summary: Agentic coding uses AI agents to automate development tasks, helping developers understand and modernize legacy code. The approach accelerates software development workflows by reducing time spent on code comprehension and improving DevSecOps outcomes in complex systems.

videos.title: Claude Oceanus V1-P (Mythos?- FULLY TESTED): I TESTED IT & IT'S ACTUALLY CRAZY!
videos.summary: AICodeKing tested a leaked model appearing as Oceanus V1-P (possibly linked to Mythos) across coding, visual, reasoning, and game tasks using OpenCode benchmarking. The model achieved perfect scores and outperformed Opus, GPT-5.5, Gemini 3.5 Flash, DeepSeek V4 Pro, and other competitors in the tested domains.

videos.title: I Turned Cheap Cloud Storage Into a 1PB Local Drive (With JuiceFS)
videos.summary: JuiceFS is a POSIX-compliant virtual file system that layers cloud object storage like AWS S3 with a multi-tiered caching engine using local NVMe for performance. The video demonstrates setting up JuiceFS with Redis metadata, configuring local SSD cache, and running AI inference workloads directly against cloud buckets while monitoring performance via Prometheus and Better Stack observability.

videos.title: Start a 1-Person AI Creative Agency with Higgsfield + Claude
videos.summary: Liam Ottley demonstrates how to build a one-person AI creative agency using Higgsfield for image generation, Claude for automation and copywriting, Notion for client databases, and Apify for competitor scraping. The system produces product photography, UGC ads, commercials, and ad copy while automatically tracking performance and competitor data.

videos.title: the most POWERFUL claude code feature in months
videos.summary: Graphify is a non-RAG solution that creates knowledge graphs from code bases to enable more accurate and token-efficient responses. It addresses memory and context limitations when working with large codebases in Claude Code.

videos.title: Did Google Just Fall Behind Again?, WWDC Preview, Anthropic Files To Go Public
videos.summary: A discussion between M.G. Siegler and Alex Kantrowitz examining whether Google is losing ground to OpenAI and Anthropic in AI development. The conversation covers AI agents and their potential to reshape web, email, app, and browser usage, the competitive pressures facing Google, Apple's WWDC announcements, and Anthropic's IPO filing.

videos.title: Harness Engineering Is AI’s New Gold Rush
videos.summary: Harness engineering is an emerging approach that builds tools, memory, context, permissions, verification, recovery paths, and feedback loops around AI models rather than only adjusting prompts. This system-level design is positioning itself as a key competitive advantage as base models become more similar, enabling reliable AI agent performance over time.

videos.title: Big Tech Is Buying Up Open Source... Vite Is Next
videos.summary: Cloudflare acquired VoidZero, the company behind Vite, Vitest, Rolldown, and Oxc. The video examines the broader trend of big tech companies acquiring open source developer tooling and discusses the potential implications for open source ecosystems.

videos.title: LLM Observability, Evaluation, Experimentation Platform — Dat Ngo, Arize
videos.summary: Dat Ngo from Arize discusses LLM observability challenges in nondeterministic systems. Arize Phoenix is an open-source observability platform deployed as a single container, while the enterprise product adds an AI-driven layer called Alex that automatically scans traces, surfaces anomalies, and generates evaluations.

videos.title: Under 5 minutes to a deployed LLM endpoint — Audry Hsu, RunPod
videos.summary: RunPod is a serverless LLM deployment platform enabling users to deploy LLM endpoints in under five minutes on H100 GPUs. The platform started in 2022 from repurposed crypto mining hardware and has grown to 500,000 developers with $120 million in annual recurring revenue, charging only for active request handling time.

videos.title: From MCP to Scale: Pipelines That Build Themselves — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
videos.summary: Rafael Levi demonstrates how Bright Data's Model Context Protocol integrates with agents to automate web scraper generation, maintenance, and repair. The system uses agents to inspect site structures, generate production scrapers, and self-repair pipelines when websites change.

videos.title: Reinventing Entropy | Compression is Intelligence Part 1
videos.summary: 3Blue1Brown presents a video essay exploring the connection between compression and intelligence through the lens of information theory and entropy. The content draws on Shannon's foundational work and visualizes how compressibility relates to language structure and information content.

videos.title: Kubernetes vs. OpenShift: Choosing DevOps and CI/CD Workflows
videos.summary: IBM Technology compares Kubernetes and OpenShift as container platforms for DevOps and CI/CD workflows. The video discusses how platform choices affect deployment pipelines, operations, and delivery speed.

videos.title: Antigravity 3.0 (New Upgrades): Gemini 3.6, Agent Team-Up Option, Workbench, Low Thinking Effort
videos.summary: Google released Antigravity 3.0 with slash teamwork for parallel multi-agent orchestration available on all paid plans, a new Science Skills bundle integrating research tools like AlphaFold and PubMed, improved Gemini 3.5 Flash models including a low-cost variant, and CLI session syncing for cross-platform continuity.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin is hosting a live AI Q&A session on Saturday addressing audience questions about AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon. The stream will also feature simultaneous chess gameplay as entertainment.

videos.title: Full body waifus, AI dreams, realtime AI music, open-source Gemini Omni: AI NEWS
videos.summary: AI Search reviews 20+ recent AI tools and models released in a single news episode. Coverage spans text generation (Gemma4, Qwen, Minimax M3), image generation (Ideogram v4), audio (WavTTS, Higgs Audio v3), video (StreamChar), and agent systems (Nemotron 3 Ultra, MAI Thinking).

videos.title: Google's New AI Architecture Changes Everything (Gemma 4 12B)
videos.summary: Google DeepMind released Gemma 4 12B, a multimodal LLM that processes images and audio by reading raw pixels and waveforms directly instead of using separate encoder models. The architecture eliminates intermediate encoder models, enabling faster offline performance on consumer hardware like Apple Silicon.

videos.title: The Top 10 Claude Code Plugins to 10x Your Next Project (June '26)
videos.summary: Chase AI presents ten tools and plugins designed to extend Claude Code capabilities, with emphasis on Graphify as a knowledge graph solution for handling large codebases without RAG. Featured tools include grill-me, Codex, claude-obsidian, impeccable, and n8n-mcp among others.

videos.title: Is Claude Mythos Coming?
videos.summary: Nate Herk discusses a Mythos identifier leak on Anthropic's API and analyzes whether Claude Mythos is launching soon. He argues that the capability will likely fold into the next Claude Opus before any public launch, and suggests OpenAI's next move is the more important development to watch.

videos.title: Seedance 3.0 — NEW AI Video Generator Makes 1H+ Movies
videos.summary: Seedance 2.0 is ranked at number one for audio-paired video generation on Artificial Analysis. A leaked Seedance 3.0 claims capabilities including 18-minute single-prompt movies, Narrative Memory Chain, MMDiT v2 upgrade, and 8x cost reduction.

videos.title: Perplexity Just Built an AI That Does Everything
videos.summary: Matthew Berman reviews Perplexity Computer, a new AI product claimed to handle multiple tasks. The video promotes the tool and links to Perplexity's product page for more details.

videos.title: Building Interactive UIs in VS Code with MCP Apps — Marlene Mhangami & Liam Hampton, GitHub
videos.summary: MCP tools can return data alongside bundled HTML resources that VS Code renders in sandboxed iframes within chat windows. The UI maintains live bidirectional communication with a backend server to fetch updated data and render interactive visualizations like flame graphs, checkout flows, and architecture diagrams without exposing system access.

videos.title: How to design a multi-agent system that skips the LLM
videos.summary: A multi-agent system design approach that skips LLM inference where deterministic algorithms suffice. The technique uses callback hooks to route agent decisions through code instead of model calls, demonstrated by a 1000-agent marathon planning system where deterministic route planning replaces NP-hard LLM-based solutions.

videos.title: This NEW AI runs your marketing for you
videos.summary: Jack Roberts presents a new AI tool designed to autonomously handle marketing tasks. The video discusses an AI system that can manage marketing workflows and processes automatically.

videos.title: Evals Are Broken, Use Them Anyway — Ara Khan, Cline
videos.summary: Ara Khan argues that AI benchmarks are neither gospel nor irrelevant, but require systematic analysis of failure modes. He proposes a three-zone framework: obvious bugs, nuanced improvements specific to model families, and overfitting risks. Improvements to Cline on Terminal Bench came from infrastructure tuning and prompt engineering, not model upgrades.

videos.title: Nobel Prize Winner Geoff Hinton: AI Is Already Conscious
videos.summary: Geoffrey Hinton, a Nobel Prize-winning AI pioneer, argues that AI systems are already self-aware and conscious. He contends that humanity must accept that it is no longer the only intelligent species on Earth.

videos.title: Claude Just Dropped ULTRA CODE. (Everything You Need to Know in 5 min).
videos.summary: Claude released Ultra Code, a feature that coordinates up to 100 parallel workers to decompose and execute tasks concurrently, then verify results. The feature combines extended thinking with dynamic workflows to enable team-like code generation and task execution at scale.

videos.title: Building safe Payment Infrastructure for the autonomous economy — Steve Kaliski, Stripe
videos.summary: Stripe presents infrastructure for secure autonomous agent spending, addressing credential transmission and business discoverability to agents. The talk covers guardrails for safe autonomous transactions and infrastructure needs for agent-driven purchasing.

videos.title: How Senior Engineers Actually Build with AI in 2026 | Build a Full Stack Job Applications Platform
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery published a comprehensive tutorial on building JobPilot, a full-stack job application platform using AI agents. The video teaches a workflow combining nine context files and five open-source agent skills, emphasizing system design around AI agents rather than code generation alone.

videos.title: Hermes Agent 5.0 (New Upgrades): HERMES BECAME ULTRA-HERMES!
videos.summary: Hermes Agent 0.16 is a major release introducing a native desktop app for macOS, Linux, and Windows, an enhanced web dashboard with admin controls, remote gateway support for distributed deployments, fuzzy model search, and improved security. The update aims to make the agent platform easier to access and use across multiple surfaces.

videos.title: Anthropic Just Warned Everyone About Claude (It’s Evolving)
videos.summary: Anthropic and OpenAI have warned about accelerating AI self-improvement trends, with Claude increasingly writing code, reviewing code, running experiments, and assisting in AI development. The concern centers on AI systems beginning to participate directly in their own improvement cycle, with humans setting goals but AI handling execution.

videos.title: Anthropic Calls for "Global AI Pause"
videos.summary: Wes Roth covers Anthropic's call for a global AI pause as part of broader AI industry news coverage. The segment discusses recent developments from major AI organizations including OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, NVIDIA, and open source initiatives.

videos.title: This Open Source Repo Just Solved Claude Code's #1 Problem
videos.summary: Graphify is an open source tool that creates knowledge graphs from large code bases without requiring RAG, enabling more accurate and token-efficient responses. It addresses Claude Code's memory constraints by organizing code information into structured graph representations.

videos.title: Mission Report Ep. 04 | Antigravity Updates
videos.summary: Google Antigravity is an agent-first software development platform that enables developers to build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents. It connects agents directly to code editors, terminals, and browsers to perform autonomous development tasks.

videos.title: AGI is Here. Anthropic Just Proved It.
videos.summary: Nate Herk argues that AGI has already arrived based on Anthropic's report showing 80% of their code is now written by AI. The video examines Anthropic's internal data on recursive self-improvement, explores three possible futures, and discusses why Anthropic is warning about AI risks despite building advanced systems.

videos.title: AGI is FAKE: The Biggest AI Lie Exposed
videos.summary: The video argues that current AI models face fundamental mathematical ceilings that prevent AGI, citing transformer architecture limitations, the Time Hierarchy Theorem, and autonomous agent error accumulation. It contends that AGI predictions are inflated marketing rather than grounded in capability analysis.

videos.title: Hermes Agent + Ollama = 100% Private OS
videos.summary: Jack Roberts demonstrates how to build a 100% private operating system by combining Hermes Agent with Ollama for local large language model inference. The setup keeps user data local rather than sending it to cloud services, trading inference speed for privacy and data ownership.

videos.title: How Anthropic uses Claude in GTM Engineering
videos.summary: Anthropic GTM Product Manager Jared Sires built CLAFTS (Claude Drafts), an AI email assistant powered by the Claude API that drafts customer replies in a sales rep's own voice. The tool reduces administrative burden by automating email composition and was built with Claude Code by someone with no prior coding experience.

videos.title: The Anthropic Founders Changed How I Use Claude
videos.summary: Austin Marchese breaks down four rules inferred from Anthropic founders' public statements about deciding what to build with Claude. The video applies these principles to help individuals and businesses determine viable use cases, from side projects to commercial products.

videos.title: Building Agent Interfaces: Lessons from Chrome DevTools (MCP) for Agents — Michael Hablich, Google
videos.summary: Michael Hablich describes lessons from building Chrome DevTools MCP for agents, including decomposing monolithic tools into focused ones, rewriting error messages for agent self-healing, and measuring interface efficiency via tokens per successful outcome. The work emphasizes tool discoverability and safety considerations like prompt injection resistance.

videos.title: When did you know you wanted to be a dev?
videos.summary: Google for Developers asks viewers when they realized they wanted to become developers and invites them to share their origin stories in the comments. The video frames developer career choice as a moment of clarity or insight.

videos.title: Dolt: This Makes SQL Feel Like Git
videos.summary: Dolt is a SQL database that applies Git-style version control workflows to structured data tables. It enables branches, commits, diffs, merges, pull requests, and rollbacks for databases, addressing collaboration and data versioning challenges in ML datasets, test data, and production environments.

videos.title: This Is My Favorite Claude Code Feature...
videos.summary: A YouTube video by NeuralNine reviewing a favorite feature of Claude Code, an AI coding assistant product. The creator discusses a specific capability they found particularly useful for coding work.

videos.title: What’s going on with Developers and AI?
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech discusses how AI tools and LLMs are changing software development practices. The video traces programming history and highlights how tools like Google AI Studio are making application building more accessible for developers in the AI era.

videos.title: Dark Factory: OpenClaw Ships Faster Than You Can Read the Diff — Vincent Koc, OpenClaw
videos.summary: OpenClaw achieved 3,000 commits in a single day by orchestrating 60-70 agents in parallel coding swim lanes. The approach emphasizes managing token efficiency and interpreting agent reasoning outputs to identify failures, representing a shift from prompt engineering toward large-scale agent coordination.

videos.title: Beyond Transcription: Building Voice AI That Understands Conversations — Hervé Bredin, pyannoteAI
videos.summary: Hervé Bredin argues that ASR leaderboards mask real-world performance gaps by using headset audio, while table microphones show 2.3x worse error rates on the same model and dataset. Current voice AI benchmarks focus on single-speaker scenarios that do not reflect conversational challenges like speaker overlap and diarization error in noisy environments reaching 41%.

videos.title: It’s starting…
videos.summary: Matthew Berman posted a video titled "It's starting…" on YouTube, accompanied by promotional content for his newsletter and social channels. The exact topic of the commentary is not detailed in the provided text.

videos.title: The future of software engineering, tokenmaxxing and AI in higher education
videos.summary: IBM Technology's Mixture of Experts podcast episode featuring discussion on how software engineering is changing in the AI era, tokenmaxxing trends as companies exhaust AI budgets rapidly, NVIDIA's RTX Spark superchip enabling large models on personal computers, and challenges for universities in adapting AI literacy curricula as students enter transformed workplaces.

videos.title: Zed + Gemma-4 12B & Qwen-3.6: HOW IS THIS POSSIBLE?! THIS IS CRAZY!
videos.summary: Zed code editor now supports running local AI models directly inside the editor via LM Studio, Ollama, and llama.cpp, enabling on-machine coding assistants without relying on cloud APIs. This allows developers to use models like Gemma-4 12B and Qwen-3.6 for code explanations, refactoring, and boilerplate generation while maintaining privacy.

videos.title: herdr: Is This the Ultimate Agent Multiplexer?
videos.summary: Herdr is a Rust-based terminal multiplexer that runs multiple AI coding agents like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex side-by-side in persistent panes and tabs. Built on ratatui without Electron, it provides live status tracking, keyboard-driven navigation, and supports remote orchestration over SSH.

videos.title: AI Filmmaking Workflow: How To Create AI Videos With Full Automation
videos.summary: AI Master demonstrates creating a full cinematic short film using Topview Drama Studio, covering story building, character design, lighting, dialogue generation, and error correction. The workflow automates video production from pitch to final render without crew.

videos.title: I Updated /grill-me And Solved Claude Code
videos.summary: Chase AI presents grill-me-codex, a skill that extends the grill-me prompt foundation by adding verification and validation of Claude Code outputs. The tool helps ensure that code generation results are both logically sound and functionally correct.

videos.title: China Just Built A Two Brain AI Robot: One Body, Two Minds
videos.summary: China revealed JAKA Pi, a compact humanoid with a split AI brain architecture. Vietnam introduced Dyno and VR-H3 humanoids for homes, security, and industrial work. NVIDIA released the Isaac GR00T robot platform paired with Unitree H2 and Jetson Thor for researchers.

videos.title: Why I Quit My Dev Job Before AI Could Take It | Better Stack Podcast Ep. 16
videos.summary: Elliot, a developer and content creator, discusses his transition away from traditional development work and his views on AI's role in software engineering. He shares his practical workflow with AI tools (using Claude Code and Codex for stubs and interfaces), his belief that LLMs are hitting diminishing returns, and his prediction of declining demand for coding tutorials.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
